reason: Book-only record: Nettles has not appeared or been named in an aired episode through 2026-07-11.

record.title: Dragonseed
record.description: Nettles is a dragonseed who appears prominently in Fire & Blood but has not yet appeared in the released episodes of House of the Dragon. In the source material she becomes famous for taming the wild dragon Sheepstealer through patience rather than traditional dragonriding methods, making her one of the most remarkable figures of the Dance of the Dragons. As of the currently released television series, Nettles has not been introduced, and no canonical TV events involving her have taken place. This entry exists for future compatibility should she appear in later seasons.

reason: Book-only record: Elmo Tully has not appeared or been named in the television series; Oscar Tully's father remains unnamed on screen.

record.title: Lord of Riverrun
record.description: Lord Elmo Tully is the heir to House Tully and later becomes Lord of Riverrun during the Dance of the Dragons. Following the decline of his aging father, Lord Grover Tully, Elmo assumes increasing responsibility for the governance of the Riverlands. He ultimately supports Queen Rhaenyra and the Black faction, bringing the strength of House Tully and many Riverlords to her cause. As one of the principal lords of central Westeros, Elmo plays an important role in organizing resistance against the Greens and maintaining unity among the often-divided Riverlands.

reason: Duplicate/non-canonical record: the aired television character is Sharako Lohar, already retained as a separate canonical record.

record.title: Triarchy Admiral
record.description: Lohar is an admiral serving the Triarchy, the alliance of the Free Cities that contests control of the Stepstones. Introduced in House of the Dragon, Lohar commands naval forces operating against Westerosi interests and becomes involved in the widening conflict surrounding the Stepstones. As tensions between the Targaryens and their enemies spread beyond Westeros, Lohar represents the growing influence of foreign powers seeking to profit from the kingdom's instability. His naval leadership contributes to the strategic importance of controlling the narrow seas during the Dance of the Dragons.
